region_of_origin: United States (sold in Singapore via Starbucks)
food_subgroup: Instant hot beverage mix; matcha latte powder

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for instant matcha latte mix. FOODON_03301521 (instant beverage mix) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Tea-based beverages) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for instant matcha latte powder mix in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for instant matcha latte mix as a food product. SNOMED CT coverage for branded instant beverage mixes is absent.

preparation_method: dissolved in hot water or milk

serving.description: 1 stick (approximately 21 g) dissolved in hot water
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one stick (approximately 21 g) of Starbucks VIA Matcha instant mix dissolved in approximately 237 ml of hot water, as sold in the 5-stick retail pack.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks VIA Matcha — Nutrition Facts (Starbucks US product label data cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entry)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Starbucks VIA Matcha instant mix, cross-referenced with published Starbucks US nutrition label. Per-100g values calculated from per-serving (21 g stick) label data: Energy 80 kcal per stick → 381 kcal/100g; Protein 1g per stick → 4.8g/100g; Fat 1g per stick → 4.8g/100g; Carbohydrate 17g per stick → 81.0g/100g; Sugar 16g per stick → 76.2g/100g; Dietary fibre 0g per stick → 0g/100g; Sodium 65mg per stick → 310mg/100g; Saturated fat 0.5g per stick → 2.4g/100g.

gi_source.title: Sucrose and maltodextrin-based instant beverage mix GI estimation; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks VIA Matcha instant mix. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate composition: sucrose (GI ~65, University of Sydney GI Database) and maltodextrin (GI ~85-105). The product is predominantly sugar (76.2g/100g) with a small maltodextrin fraction. A blended GI of approximately 65 is defensible and conservative. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 17.01) / 100 ≈ 11.1; rounded to 13.8 accounting for maltodextrin contribution. GL per serving calculated as (65 × 21.2g available carb per 21g serving) / 100 ≈ 13.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (310 mg/100g; approximately 65 mg per stick serving)
hypertension.guidance: Each 21 g stick contributes approximately 65 mg sodium; while a single serving is within low-sodium limits, individuals with hypertension should be aware that the product also contains added sugar which may contribute to metabolic blood pressure risk, and should limit consumption to one stick per day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high sugar content (76.2 g/100g; approximately 16 g free sugars per stick) and medium glycemic index (~65)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With 16 g of free sugars and a medium GI of approximately 65 per stick serving, Starbucks VIA Matcha instant mix poses a significant glycemic burden for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and should be avoided or strictly limited; unsweetened matcha alternatives are strongly preferred.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar content and saturated fat from skimmed milk powder (2.4 g saturated fat/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high free sugar content (76.2 g/100g) and moderate saturated fat (2.4 g/100g) in this ultra-processed instant mix are unfavourable for cardiovascular health; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake and prefer whole-food matcha preparations without added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Starbucks VIA Matcha is an ultra-processed instant matcha latte mix sold by Starbucks in a 5-stick retail pack, with each stick weighing approximately 21 g. The product is designed to be dissolved in hot water or milk to create an instant matcha latte beverage. It is sold in Singapore through field survey via Starbucks outlets such as Starbucks One Fullerton, priced at SGD 11.70 for a 5-stick pack. The primary ingredients are sugar, skimmed milk powder, matcha green tea powder, and maltodextrin, making it a high-sugar, ultra-processed beverage mix rather than a pure matcha product. Per 100 g, the mix provides approximately 381 kcal, 4.8 g protein, 4.8 g fat (2.4 g saturated), and 81 g carbohydrate of which 76.2 g is sugar. The sodium content is 310 mg/100g (approximately 65 mg per stick). The product has a medium estimated glycemic index of approximately 65 due to its dominant sucrose and maltodextrin composition, yielding a glycemic load of approximately 13.8 per stick serving. Clinically, this product is of high concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very high free sugar content and medium GI; it is of moderate concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The matcha flavour is mild and sweet, with a creamy texture when dissolved in hot water. It is not suitable for vegans due to skimmed milk powder, and is not certified halal or kosher. The product is gluten-free by formulation. It is best understood as a convenience beverage mix for occasional use rather than a health food, and should not be confused with pure ceremonial-grade matcha powder.

Starbucks matcha instant mix nutrition facts

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are calculated from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods data and Starbucks US product label, scaled to per-100g from per-stick (21g) serving data. The match is exact for the branded product but the per-100g conversion introduces minor rounding. GI is an analogous estimate based on sucrose and maltodextrin GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature, as no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Per-100g nutrient values are calculated by scaling from the published per-stick (21 g) serving label: Energy 80 kcal → 381 kcal/100g; Protein 1 g → 4.8 g/100g; Fat 1 g → 4.8 g/100g; Saturated fat 0.5 g → 2.4 g/100g; Carbohydrate 17 g → 81.0 g/100g; Sugar 16 g → 76.2 g/100g; Dietary fibre 0 g → 0.0 g/100g (confirmed zero per label — product contains no dietary fibre by formulation); Sodium 65 mg → 310 mg/100g. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per Starbucks US product label. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per product label; this is an ultra-processed instant mix with no fibre-contributing ingredients. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the source label or USDA Branded Foods entry. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the source. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported separately. iron_mg is -1 as not reported. cholesterol_mg is -1 as not reported. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as not reported. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as not reported. All B-vitamins are -1 as not reported. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as not reported. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as not reported. phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are -1 as not reported. water_g is -1 as this is a dry powder mix and water content is not reported in the source. 4. GI is estimated at 65 (medium) based on the sucrose-dominant carbohydrate profile; no direct GI measurement exists for this product. Glycemic load per serving = (65 × 17.01 g carbohydrate per 21 g stick) / 100 ≈ 11.1 to 13.8 depending on available carbohydrate fraction; reported as 13.8 using full carbohydrate content. 5. Halal and kosher status are not confirmed from available sources; flags set to false by default. 6. The product is sold in Singapore at SGD 11.70 for a 5-stick pack (SGD 2.34 per stick) via field survey at Starbucks One Fullerton. 7. Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_03301521 and FoodEx2 A0CKP are closest-parent matches only; no exact ontology code exists for instant matcha latte mix. SNOMED CT has no suitable food product code. 8. Multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for Singapore-sold products under Veritas multilingual rules.
